value.market_read: WTI Curve: WTI settled at $70.24 in the prior session, firming after the latest WPSR (week ending June 19, released Wed Jun 24) showed the reported Cushing draw that tightened prompt balances and supported the front month into today's close. The curve is in clean backwardation, with M1 at $70.24 sliding to roughly $66.71 at the May 2027 tenor before a slight back-end uptick. The M1–M12 spread sits at $3.25, a moderate backwardation that prices near-term prompt tightness rather than a surplus/storage play. The M1–M2 spread of about 36c reinforces a firm prompt without signaling acute scarcity. Watch: Monitor Cushing balances in the next WPSR — continued draws would steepen front-end backwardation, while a build would flatten the prompt. Brent Curve: Brent settled at $73.57 in the prior session, holding a $3.33 premium to WTI. The curve is backwardated but choppy, with a notable kink mid-curve (Jan 2027 near $71.39 before back-month contracts rebound toward $73.25) reflecting thin liquidity and seasonal positioning in deferred tenors. The M1–M12 spread of $2.90 is slightly flatter than WTI's, implying global prompt tightness is real but marginally less pronounced than the US domestic picture. The relatively narrow Brent–WTI spread points to soft US export arb economics. Watch: Watch the back-end Brent kink and whether prompt waterborne demand pulls the front structure steeper. RBOB Gasoline Curve: RBOB settled at $2.8529 in the prior session, the strongest point on a steeply backwardated summer-demand curve. The front drops sharply to roughly $2.18 by the winter contracts before the spring 2027 contracts rebound toward $2.39 — the classic summer-to-winter seasonal roll as the market prices peak driving-season pull now and weaker barrels into winter blendstock. The M1–M12 spread of 46c signals strong prompt gasoline tightness consistent with mid-summer demand. The steep prompt structure supports gasoline cracks and refinery incentive to maximize gasoline yield. Watch: Monitor gasoline demand and inventory draws into the July 4 driving peak — any demand miss would collapse the front of the curve fastest. ULSD Diesel Curve: ULSD settled at $3.1319 in the prior session, leading a smoothly backwardated distillate curve. Unlike gasoline, the ULSD curve declines monotonically from $3.13 to $2.67 across the strip with no seasonal rebound, reflecting persistently tight prompt distillate balances rather than a sharp seasonal switch. The M1–M12 spread of 46c mirrors gasoline's steepness, signaling firm prompt distillate demand and lean inventories. The consistent backwardation keeps diesel cracks well-supported and discourages storage economics. Watch: Watch distillate inventory builds and any softening in industrial/freight demand that could ease the tight prompt structure heading out of summer.
value.kpi_wti: WTI settled at $70.24, firming on the prior session after the latest WPSR's reported Cushing draw tightened prompt balances.
value.kpi_brent: Brent settled at $73.57, holding a modest premium over WTI with a backwardated but choppy mid-curve structure.
value.kpi_rbob: RBOB settled at $2.8529, anchoring a steeply backwardated curve as peak summer driving-season demand pulls the prompt higher.
value.kpi_ulsd: ULSD settled at $3.1319, leading a smoothly backwardated distillate curve that signals persistently tight prompt balances.
value.kpi_bw: The Brent–WTI spread at $3.33 is relatively narrow, implying weak US export arb economics that keep barrels domestic and risk pressuring WTI lower to re-widen the spread.
value.kpi_curve_regime: All four curves are in backwardation with M1–M12 spreads of $2.90–$3.25 on crude and ~46c on products, pricing genuine prompt tightness rather than a contango surplus/storage play.
